objective: Define the minimum set of M365-only changes required to make the current M365 capability commercially honest, operationally supportable, and enterprise-ready as a standalone deterministic module ASAP.

completed.summary: Locked the standalone M365 v1 commercial boundary to the 9 implemented instruction actions in docs/commercialization/m365-v1-supported-surface.md and explicitly excluded the remaining 251 planned actions plus the 5 notebook/spec-only group actions from launch claims.

Defined the standalone buyer, operator, positioning, and deployment model in docs/commercialization/m365-v1-positioning-and-north-star-delta.md and synchronized Operations/NORTHSTAR.md so the broader AI Workforce vision no longer overclaims the standalone M365 v1 commercial boundary.

Inventoried the current config-loading surfaces, locked tenant YAML plus env-assisted secret fallback as the canonical production contract in docs/commercialization/m365-canonical-config-contract.md, and narrowed the env docs so .env is no longer described as the production authority.

Defined the migration path, auth-mode decision matrix, secret policy, certificate guidance, and legacy-surface deprecation map in docs/commercialization/m365-config-migration-and-auth-policy.md. The supported posture is now singular: tenant YAML selected by UCP_TENANT is the production authority, app_only is the default production auth mode, certificate auth is enterprise-preferred, client secret is transitional only, and delegated auth is local and test only.

Defined the audit-event model, governance-evidence model, current gap map, and enterprise acceptance boundary in docs/commercialization/m365-audit-and-governance-evidence-model.md. The repo now explicitly distinguishes the formally evidenced instruction API audit surface from the broader but only partially evidenced ops-adapter and admin audit surfaces, and documents snapshot_mode admin audit as a real commercialization gap.

Defined the permission-tier posture, approval-boundary model, fail-closed rules, and exception and escalation boundary in docs/commercialization/m365-permission-approval-fail-closed-hardening.md. The repo now explicitly documents that permission tiers and ops-adapter approvals exist, but permissive tier fallbacks, OPA fail-open paths, and approval-less instruction-API mutations remain hardening gaps rather than enterprise-ready claims.

commercial_gap_summary: marketable capability boundary is not yet locked to the proven v1 surface
production configuration is split between tenant config and direct .env loading
some validation evidence can still pass in mock mode
enterprise audit posture is incomplete for governance review
packaging, onboarding, and collateral are not yet unified into one sellable path

in_scope: define and document the commercially supported M365 v1 action surface
establish one canonical production configuration and identity model
harden governance, audit, and fail-closed behavior
produce live-tenant validation evidence and release-gate criteria
define packaging, onboarding, runbooks, and support boundaries for a standalone M365 module
update Operations/NORTHSTAR.md if commercialization scope changes materially
out_of_scope: implementing the remaining planned capability universe purely to claim breadth
broad SAID or UCP rework outside direct M365 dependency requirements
new inference algorithms or unrelated mathematical systems
unapproved tenant-impacting provisioning beyond later approved validation scope

phases.name: Commercial Boundary Lock
phases.goal: Make the product claim commercially honest before broadening scope.

subphases.name: Supported Surface and Non-Goals Lock
subphases.goal: Lock the commercially supported M365 v1 surface to the actions that are currently proven and supportable.
outputs: supported action matrix for M365 v1
product-claim boundary language tied to current evidence
evidence-source map for each supported action

subphases.name: Buyer and Operator Positioning and North Star Delta
subphases.goal: Define who this standalone module is for, how it is positioned, and whether North Star requires synchronization.
outputs: North Star delta review and update decision

phases.name: Canonical Config and Identity Model
phases.goal: Eliminate production ambiguity around credentials, tenant selection, auth mode, and org mappings.

subphases.name: Config Inventory and Canonical Target Contract
subphases.goal: Inventory the current configuration and identity loading paths and define the one canonical production contract.

subphases.name: Config Migration and Auth and Secret Policy
subphases.goal: Define how the repo moves from the current state to the canonical production contract without ambiguity.
outputs: migration path away from direct production .env dependence
deprecation map for legacy loading patterns

phases.name: Governance and Audit Hardening
phases.goal: Bring the runtime to an enterprise-reviewable governance posture.

subphases.name: Audit and Governance Evidence Model
subphases.goal: Define the audit and governance evidence required for enterprise review of the M365 runtime.
outputs: gap map for snapshot or partial audit surfaces

subphases.name: Permission, Approval, and Fail-Closed Hardening
subphases.goal: Define the hardening expectations for permission tiers, approvals, and fail-closed behavior.

phases.name: Live Tenant Evidence and Release Gates
phases.goal: Replace marketing ambiguity with real acceptance evidence.

subphases.name: Live-Tenant Validation Matrix
subphases.goal: Define exactly what must be validated against a live tenant to support enterprise release claims.

subphases.name: Release Gates and Certification Decision Model
subphases.goal: Convert evidence requirements into explicit release and certification decisions.

phases.name: Packaging, Install, and Operator Onboarding
phases.goal: Make the module deployable and operable without SmartHaus hand-holding.

subphases.name: Packaging, Install, and Bootstrap
subphases.goal: Define how the standalone module is packaged, installed, and bootstrapped.

subphases.name: Operator Onboarding, Runbooks, and Support Boundary
subphases.goal: Define the operator experience after installation.

phases.name: Enterprise Collateral and Pilot Acceptance
phases.goal: Make the product sellable and reviewable.

subphases.name: Enterprise Collateral Pack
subphases.goal: Produce the buyer-facing and review-facing collateral for the standalone module.
outputs: supported-action matrix for sales and delivery

subphases.name: Pilot Acceptance and Customer Handoff
subphases.goal: Define how SmartHaus and the customer determine a successful pilot and handoff.

validation: Operations/EXECUTION_PLAN.md references this initiative and plan ID
the plan md, yaml, and json files remain consistent
the Codex prompt pair exists and points to this plan
future implementation phases define explicit validation and acceptance evidence before execution
enterprise readiness claims must separate mock evidence from live-tenant evidence
success_criteria: SmartHaus can describe exactly what the standalone M365 module v1 does and does not do
production configuration is defined by one canonical contract
enterprise governance and audit expectations are explicit and testable
release acceptance requires live-tenant evidence for enterprise-critical surfaces
packaging, onboarding, and collateral support a real pilot or enterprise buyer review
dependencies: existing M365 instruction contract and MA verification set in docs/contracts and configs/generated
existing TAI licensed module model in docs/TAI_LICENSED_MODULE_MODEL.md
UCP tenant configuration conventions where shared governance patterns apply
